a.label: Compatibility point
a.standard_term: common zero locus of a finite system of constraint functions

b.label: Regular compatibility point
b.standard_term: transverse intersection point (regular value, full-rank Jacobian)

evidence.label: Proposition (Universal Singularity Structure)
evidence.summary:
Let p be a regular compatibility point and q = (p, 0) ∈ Rⁿ⁺¹.

**(i)** q is a singular point of M: ∇F_joint(q) = 0.

**(ii)** The Hessian H = ∇²F_joint(q) has block structure:
```
H = [ 2 JᵀJ    0  ]
    [   0      -2  ]

evidence.label: Regular compatibility point
evidence.summary: The point is **regular** if the k × n Jacobian J = [∂Fᵢ/∂xⱼ] has full row rank k at p (transverse intersection).
